Skip to contents
jstats 0.9.80
  • Reference

Internal: build jsave's error message for unsupported column types

dot-jst_jsave_unsupported_type_error_msg.Rd

The statistical interchange formats (.sav, .dta, .xpt) cannot store complex, list, raw, or POSIXlt columns; the underlying writers abort mid-write with a low-level message that does not name the offending column (e.g. "Columns of type complex not supported yet", or "...type list..." for a POSIXlt column, which is list-backed). This helper produces one clean package-level error that names the column(s) and their type(s) and gives the right remedy for each: complex/list/raw have no sensible conversion and are dropped, while POSIXlt converts faithfully to POSIXct (the same instant, which the formats can store).

Usage

.jst_jsave_unsupported_type_error_msg(vars, types, ext, data_name)

Arguments

vars

Character vector of offending column names.

types

Character vector of the columns' types, parallel to vars ("complex", "list", "raw", or "POSIXlt").

ext

The target extension ("sav", "dta", or "xpt").

data_name

The data frame's name, used in the suggested fix code.

Value

Character scalar suitable for stop(call. = FALSE).

On this page
